summary: Scorching triple Playing In The Band suite with soaring Bird Song
blurb: The concert opens with a high‑energy Gimme Some Lovin' and builds through a powerful first set highlighted by Bird Song. A triple Playing In The Band sequence, China Doll jam and soaring She Belongs To Me encore make the second set unforgettable. Jerry’s fiery guitar and Brent’s intense keys drive the performance.
review:
On April 28 1985 at Frost Amphitheatre, the Dead delivered a night that still feels fresh in the tapes. The first set bursts out with a ripping Gimme Some Lovin' that rolls straight into Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo and New Minglewood Blues before the band lifts off on Bird Song – a performance many reviewers called “soaring.”  The second set is where the magic really erupts: three distinct Playing In The Band sections bookend an inspired China Doll jam, and the rhythm section drives the music through Drums‑Space back into another massive PITB climax.  Jerry Garcia’s guitar work is described as “fiery,” “smoking,” and “on fire” throughout, compensating for any vocal roughness, while Phil Lesh provides a powerful bass foundation that locks in with the twin drummers.  Brent Mydland’s keyboards take listeners on a serious voyage, especially during the extended jams, and both Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann lay down solid, dynamic grooves.  The show closes on an emotional high with U.S. Blues and a heartfelt She Belongs To Me encore that many fans cite as one of the season’s highlights.

For listeners seeking the clearest window into this performance, the Miller soundboard (gd85-04-28.sbd.new.miller.28357.sbeok.flacf) is the stand‑out source – it captures the full set with crisp detail, no dropouts, and a balanced mix that lets every instrument shine.  Whether you’re chasing Jerry’s blistering solos or Brent’s lush keys, this recording delivers the most faithful representation of what made the Frost show so memorable.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across nine of twelve recordings, with consistent praise for Jerry's guitar, the triple Playing In The Band suite, and Brent's keyboards; only one negative and two mixed reviews, so a robust +1.6 adjustment is warranted.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture – crisp, balanced mix with no dropouts; showcases the full set and all standout moments clearly.

key_highlights: Triple Playing In The Band suite with an epic China Doll jam
Soaring Bird Song that lifts the first set
High‑energy Gimme Some Lovin' opener and powerful U.S. Blues/She Belongs To Me encore
Crisp soundboard recording that lets Jerry's fiery guitar and Brent's intense keys shine

band_performance.Jerry: Fiery, smoking solos and authoritative guitar work dominate the night; occasional vocal roughness is more than made up for by his on‑point playing.
band_performance.Phil: All‑star bass lines provide a powerful, tight foundation that locks in with the drums and propels the extended jams.
band_performance.Bob: Passionate rhythm guitar and strong vocals, especially on Wharf Rat, add energy and depth throughout the set.
band_performance.Brent: Intense keyboard voyages accent the jams; while his mix level is sometimes low, his playing remains impressive and perfectly complements the final encore.
band_performance.Mickey: Solid drumming with good dynamics, driving the second‑set jams and contributing to a tight Drum/Space segment.
band_performance.Billy: Provides a steady groove that underpins the extended Playing In The Band suite and keeps the energy high during the jam sections.
